mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate and raises the present value of future corporate cash flows, mechanically producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates above 9.5% indicate severe slack in the labor market that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized recovery in consumer and residential demand that is stronger than either sector alone, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term does not significantly improve predictive power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is constrained near zero, higher CPI inflation signals successful reflation and nominal growth recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premium compresses.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL do not coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects manufacturing sector expansion and inventory restocking that boosts corporate revenues, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payroll employment indicates contracting labor income and falling aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and payroll employment confirms a broad-based demand recovery spanning both household spending and labor income, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days that exceeds either single-series signal.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (t-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units signal a deeply impaired residential construction sector and incomplete recovery from the housing bust, creating near-term negative risk sentiment that drags S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.
